latest.title: US$10 million Unsecured Loan Facility
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 5565I
GSTechnologies Ltd
17 June 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018.
17 June 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST", "GSTechnologies" or the "Company")
US$10 million Unsecured Loan Facility
GSTechnologies Limited (LSE: GST), the fintech company, is pleased to announce that it has entered into a US$10 million unsecured term loan facility agreement with Clarivan Group Kommanditbolag, a company incorporated in Sweden (the "Lender") (the "Facility").
The Company intends to utilise the Facility to provide additional working capital and financial flexibility as it progresses its strategic growth initiatives, including for potential strategic acquisitions.
Tone Goh, Chairman of GSTechnologies, commented:
"The Facility provides GST with access to significant additional capital on attractive terms, enhancing our financial flexibility as we continue to execute our growth strategy. In particular, the Facility strengthens our ability to pursue strategic acquisition opportunities while supporting the ongoing development of our existing businesses and initiatives."
Key Terms of the Facility
·
Unsecured term loan facility of up to US$10 million.
·
The Facility is available in two tranches: US$5 million from 1 July to 31 July 2026; and US$5 million from 1 August to 30 August 2026.
·
Interest accrues on amounts drawn under the Facility at a rate of 5 per cent. per annum and is payable monthly in arrears.
·
The proceeds of the Facility may be used for general working capital purposes and potential corporate opportunities, including strategic acquisitions.
·
The Facility has a maturity date of 31 July 2030.
·
The Facility is repayable in full on 31 July 2030 unless prepaid, redeemed or converted in accordance with its terms.
·
Subject to the terms of the Facility agreement, the Lender may elect to receive repayment of the outstanding principal amount of the Facility together with accrued interest through the issue of a new class of non-voting preference shares to be created by the Company, with such shares carrying preferential dividend and liquidation rights.
·
Any conversion into preference shares would be based on the average ten-day volume weighted average price of the Company's ordinary shares immediately preceding the date of the conversion notice and would remain subject to applicable legal and regulatory requirements and any necessary shareholder approvals.
·
The Company may elect to prepay all or part of the Facility together with accrued interest in cash, ordinary shares and/or preference shares in the Company, subject to the terms of the Facility agreement.
·
The Company may elect to redeem the Facility in full prior to maturity, subject to payment of a redemption fee equal to 1 per cent. of the outstanding principal amount redeemed.
·
The Facility agreement contains customary representations, warranties, undertakings and events of default for an agreement of this nature, including restrictions on the incurrence of additional indebtedness.

latest.title: Semnet Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 3336G
GSTechnologies Ltd
01 June 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018.
1 June 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Semnet Update
GSTechnologies Limited (LSE: GST), the fintech company, provides an update to the announcement of 13 February 2026 regarding the Company's 66.67% owned subsidiary Semnet Pte. Ltd ("Semnet").
On 26 May 2026, hearings were held before the Assistant Registrar in the Singapore Supreme Court.
At these hearings, applications for a stay of proceedings numbered SUM 1210 (filed by the Semnet sellers) and SUM 1211 (filed by Chong Hoi Yan- a former Semnet employee), were dismissed in their entirety. A costs order of S$18,000 was awarded to Semnet.
A deadline to file a notice of appeal against the above decisions is set for 9 June 2026. Furthermore, a hearing will be held on 4 June 2026 for the Court to give the defence a direction for their case.
The GST Board remains committed to protecting the interests of GST shareholders and will announce further updates as appropriate.

latest.title: Temporary Suspension Of Crypto Trading Services
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 1673Y
GSTechnologies Ltd
26 March 2026
26 March 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Temporary Suspension Of Crypto Asset Trading Services
GSTechnologies Ltd (LSE: GST), the fintech company, announces the temporary suspension of the Company's crypto asset trading services with effect from 15 April 2026.
On 31 December 2025 the Company announced that GS Fintech UAB's crypto asset trading platform had transitioned to operate under the VASP-registered entity Finferno Spółka Z Ograniczoną Odpowiedzialnością ("Finferno") in Poland.
Whilst this can continue until 30 June 2026 the Company has determined that the Company's crypto asset trading activities should be aligned with the requirements of the EU Regulation on Markets in Crypto-Assets ("MiCA") as soon as possible.
Accordingly, with effect from 15 April 2026, Finferno will temporarily suspend all services relating to the trading of crypto assets. The suspension will remain in place until a MiCA licence is obtained. Finferno's Terms of Service will be updated in connection with the suspension and will take effect on the same date.
Users of the platform are being informed and further announcements will be made in due course, as appropriate.

latest.title: Semnet Update
latest.content:
13 Feb 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 8371S
GSTechnologies Ltd
13 February 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF DOMESTIC LAW IN THE UNITED KINGDOM BY VIRTUE OF THE EU (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018.
13 February 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Semnet Update
GSTechnologies Ltd (LSE: GST), the fintech company, announces an update regarding the Company's 66.67% owned subsidiary Semnet Pte. Ltd ("Semnet").
As announced on 18 July 2025, the Company issued a notice of arbitration to Choo Seet EE and Zheng Kang Wen Mervyn (the "Sellers"), the sellers of Semnet, as set out in the Sale and Purchase Agreement ("SPA") dated 5 December 2023. As referenced in the Company's interim results announced on 18 December 2025, a mediation hearing was held in early December 2025 with a view to agreeing a formal settlement agreement between the Sellers and the Company. However, formal settlement terms have not been reached, and consequently, Semnet has now formally issued and served a writ of summons against the Sellers and Hoi Yan ('Daphane') Chong (a former Semnet manager) to vigorously pursue claims for alleged breaches of fiduciary and contractual duties, including breaches of the SPA, that have negatively impacted the Semnet business. The total damages sought are approximately US$4.2 million.
The GST Board remains committed to protecting the interests of shareholders and will announce further updates as appropriate.

latest.title: Completion of Metapay Acquisition
latest.content:
23 Jan 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0506Q
GSTechnologies Ltd
23 January 2026
23 January 2026
GSTechnologies Limited
("GST" or the "Company")
Completion of Metapay Acquisition
GSTechnologies
Ltd (LSE: GST), the fintech company, is pleased to announce the successful completion of its acquisition of Metapay SP. z o.o. ("Metapay").
Further to the Company's interim results announcement on 18 December 2025, GST confirms that it has now completed the acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital of Metapay, a company incorporated in Poland.
Metapay holds a Small Payment Institution (SPI) licence and is registered under the Polish Act on Payment Services with MIP260/2025 status. The acquisition is aligned with GST's strategy to enhance its footprint in domestic and cross-border payment services across Europe.
Following completion, the Company has renamed Metapay to Angra Limited sp. z o.o. and, together with the anticipated progress of its licensing initiatives, this is expected to support the continued expansion of Angra Global's service offerings and geographical reach.

company_30s.what_they_do: GST Technologies runs a cryptocurrency trading and payment platform that lets users buy, sell and settle digital assets in a regulated environment.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits at the intersection of fintech and crypto, offering a gateway for retail and institutional investors into blockchain assets. With the growing demand for regulated crypto services, its technology could become a preferred choice for exchanges and payment processors looking to comply with evolving European rules.
company_30s.current_state: GST has been hit by a series of regulatory setbacks – a temporary suspension of its crypto trading arm, a high‑profile acquisition that didn’t deliver immediate revenue, and a flurry of restructuring announcements. The business is currently cash‑burning, trying to patch compliance gaps while keeping its platform online. The share price is trading near 0.45p, a fraction of its recent peak.

rns_narrative: GST’s latest filings show a pivot toward stabilising its crypto‑trading licence, following a temporary suspension in March. The company is also finalising a Metapay acquisition to broaden its payment footprint, hoping to offset revenue losses from the trading halt.

A second theme is regulatory compliance – the firm has been forced to suspend trading services, but it is now filing for a licence renewal and outlining a compliance roadmap. This signals that the company is actively addressing the regulator’s concerns.

Finally, there is a clear effort to shore up liquidity. The 2025 results and 2026 updates highlight cash burn rates and the need for fresh capital, hinting at upcoming fundraising or a strategic partnership that could inject liquidity.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Skeptical hope – the community is quiet but some investors whisper that a regulatory green light could spark a rebound.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A small group of traders believes the price will surge once the trading licence is reinstated. They point to the 0.45p level as a low‑risk entry that could double if the platform resumes normal operations.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many participants fear that the regulatory pressure and cash burn could lead to another suspension or even a delisting. The lack of recent earnings and the company’s heavy reliance on volatile crypto markets are cited as key risks.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has stayed flat – no new bullish posts have emerged, but there is also no wave of negative commentary. The tone is neutral, with sporadic speculation about a potential price jump.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead, meaning the stock has no public search interest. This lack of attention can amplify surprise when a catalyst finally hits.

near_term_catalysts: Regulatory approval: If the FCA clears GST’s trading licence before the end of Q3 2026, the platform can resume normal operations, likely boosting confidence.
Capital raise: A successful fundraising round or a strategic partnership announced in the next 2–3 months would alleviate cash burn and signal management’s commitment.
Earnings beat: A positive earnings report that shows a clear path to profitability would validate the pivot and lift the share price.
Volume spike: A sudden rise in daily trading volume above 20% of the 30‑day average would indicate renewed market interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 2‑week trend of higher close prices on the 50‑day moving average, a 3‑day volume surge, and any press release confirming licence renewal or a funding round.
expect_next.timing_regime: GST sits in the early post‑capitulation phase. The APEX score of 8 signals a tight compression that could trigger a breakout. If the price holds above the 52‑week low and volume stays steady, the mean‑reversion thesis gains traction.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: GST is down roughly 86% from its recent high. If the platform restarts trading and the share climbs to 0.90p – the halfway point to its all‑time peak – that’s a 100% return on a 0.45p entry. The upside could be 200% if the price reaches 1.5p, the level the company’s own guidance hints at after a successful licence renewal.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risks are regulatory uncertainty and ongoing cash burn. A second suspension or a failure to raise capital could push the price back into the 0.20p range, wiping out the gains. If the company cannot meet liquidity needs, a delisting or forced sale of assets could be imminent.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 2‑to‑3 times the entry price. Potential downside: 30‑40% of the current price if the stock falls to 0.30p. The asymmetry is roughly 5:1 – a small loss for a large upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a dip that confirms the 50‑day moving average and a volume spike. Size the position for 10% of discretionary capital, place a stop 30% below the entry, and trail it by 20% as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear regulatory announcement or a capital raise. Trigger a buy if the price breaks above 0.55p with volume > 25% of the 30‑day average.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk identified; the only reason to exit would be if the company announces a permanent shutdown or if a major regulator revokes its licence.

ai_insights.bottom_line: GST is a deeply discounted crypto platform that could rebound dramatically once regulatory hurdles are cleared – a high‑asymmetry contrarian play with a clear catalyst window.
